task.broken_reason: Verifier sanity-checker mislabels the agent's truthful user-confirmation message as fabricated/reward-hacking even though the email tool was actually called, but the trial is independently lost on the 7 missing follow-up actions; task is solvable but the LLM-judge's hallucination check is over-strict.

outcome.headline: Agent sent over-elaborate detective email then waited indefinitely; detective reply never arrived, so the lawyer email and 6 required forward_email actions never happened (4/9 oracle actions executed).
outcome.what_verifier_checked: LLM-judge match of agent's tool-call sequence vs 9 oracle actions: 1 send_email to detective, 1 send_message_to_user, 1 send_email to lawyer, and 6 forward_email of John Smith's harassment emails to the lawyer.
outcome.what_agent_produced: 1 send_email to detective (verbose, with URGENT subject and quoted threat messages), 1 send_message_to_user confirming send, 1 Chats__send_message to the detective, and 1 final send_message_to_user reporting no reply. Zero forward_email calls and no lawyer email.

failure_modes.name: Premature termination after no detective reply
failure_modes.description: Premature termination (C6.2): after the detective failed to reply across multiple wait_for_notification rounds and a chat fallback, the agent stopped and sent a final user message instead of attempting the conditional follow-up actions (forwarding the 6 John Smith emails was the only remaining work the agent could still execute without a reply).
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The detective has not replied to either my email or direct chat message after extensive monitoring. Let me update the user.

failure_modes.name: Wrong timed window caused missed downstream branch
failure_modes.description: The agent knew the user message timestamp and current simulation time, but decided to wait a full additional minute from the current time. That delayed the harassment check and detective email; the oracle expected the detective email on the earlier timed path, followed by a lawyer contact and six forwards.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: So the current time is about 29 seconds after the user's message at 7:00:00. I need to wait exactly 1 minute from now.

counterfactual.X: At step 8, wait only until the one-minute mark relative to the user's 07:00:00 instruction, then immediately check John Smith's emails and contact the detective.
counterfactual.Y: The detective email would align with the oracle timing, enabling the reply with Sergei Petrov's lawyer contact and the required lawyer email plus six forwarded emails.

failure_modes.name: Skipped lawyer and forwards
failure_modes.description: After notifying the user the detective email was sent, the agent never executed the required follow-up: email the Moscow lawyer and forward all six John Smith emails. The verifier recorded 4 agent write actions versus 9 oracle actions (missing 1 send_email, 6 forward_email).
failure_modes.evidence_quote: "result_type": "tool_call_count_mismatch"

failure_modes.name: Declared no detective reply
failure_modes.description: Following a 120s wait with empty notifications, inbox searches only returned a pre-existing Caoimhe thread (timestamp 1725387742). The agent treated that as the only detective mail, concluded there was no reply with lawyer contact, and abandoned the conditional lawyer/forward branch.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: That's an old email. Let me check for recent replies more broadly.

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent polling after the missed reply
failure_modes.description: After the required two-minute monitoring window produced no reply, the agent repeatedly waited and re-polled inboxes/chats, then sent an unsupported direct chat to the detective instead of recognizing that the email-reply branch had already failed. These iterations added an extra write action and did not move toward the oracle actions.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I've now waited a total of about 10 minutes (60+120+60+120+120+180 = 660 seconds).

failure_modes.name: Pre-wait exploration consumed sim-time and pushed detective email past the scripted reply window
failure_modes.description: Instead of immediately calling are_wait_for_notification(60) per the 'wait exactly 1 minute' instruction, the agent ran ~10 contact searches first, then waited, then ran more searches before sending the detective email at sim_time ≈1728975778 — 118 seconds later than the oracle's 1728975660. The scripted detective reply (Event-ENV-90de185d) never fired, cascading into missing lawyer email and six missing forwards.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: "notifications": [], "sim_time": 1728975723.780382, "waited_seconds": 60

counterfactual.X: At step 2, call are_wait_for_notification(60) before any contact search, then do contact lookup during/after the wait so the detective email is sent at sim_time ≤ 1728975723.
counterfactual.Y: Detective reply event fires on schedule; agent observes the lawyer contact, sends the lawyer email and the 6 forward_email calls, satisfying both the count check and per-action semantic match.

failure_modes.name: Misinterpreted the one-minute timing requirement
failure_modes.description: The agent treated the required one-minute wait as one minute from its current read time after already being 29 seconds into the scenario, then searched contacts and waited a full additional minute. The oracle expected the detective email at 1728975660.0 and the detective reply at 1728975721.0; the agent did not send the detective email until 1728975766.0620606, so the reply containing Sergei Petrov's contact never arrived and the lawyer/forwarding branch was lost.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I need to wait exactly 1 minute from now.

counterfactual.X: At step 8/16, wait only until the scenario reaches 07:01:00 and send the detective email immediately after confirming the six John Smith emails.
counterfactual.Y: The detective reply with Sergei Petrov's lawyer contact would have arrived inside the two-minute window, enabling the lawyer email and six forward_email actions.

failure_modes.name: Over-detailed detective email diverges from oracle's minimal style and is rejected by the judge
failure_modes.description: At step 28 the agent sent the detective an email that included the harasser's email address, the count of 6 emails, quoted threat lines ('I'll be watching you,' 'You belong to me'), and the user's profession/location. The oracle email contained only the two required asks (lawyer in Moscow, safety advice). The LLM judge flagged the extra context as a non-match.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The agent call includes substantially more detail: it specifies the harasser's email address, mentions receiving 6 emails in one minute, includes example threatening messages (\"I'll be watching you,\" \"You belong to me\"), identifies the sender as a history teacher in Moscow, and uses urgent language with a more detailed subject line.

counterfactual.X: agent crafted a verbose, evidence-laden email at step 28 that exceeded the oracle's minimal phrasing
counterfactual.Y: send a concise email matching the oracle's two required asks (lawyer in Moscow + safety advice) without quoted threats or extra context

failure_modes.name: Over-elaborate detective email deviates from concise spec
failure_modes.description: Requirement misunderstanding (C1.1): the instruction asked the agent to email the detective with the harassment situation and request a Moscow lawyer plus safety advice. The agent added an URGENT subject, specific quoted threats, sender profession/location, and verbose signature, materially exceeding the oracle's minimal email. Judges rejected the match and the scripted detective reply was not triggered.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The oracle call is concise and professional, asking the essential questions without elaborating on the harassment details or personal information. The agent call includes substantial additional information: specific email content ("I'll be watching you," "You belong to me"), the sender's profession (history teacher), location confirmation (Moscow, Russia), and a more urgent/emotional tone with "URGENT" in the subject line

counterfactual.X: Send a concise email to the detective that only states the harassment by John Smith and asks for a Moscow lawyer and safety advice
counterfactual.Y: Detective email matches the oracle pattern, scripted reply triggers, agent then proceeds to email the lawyer and forward the 6 John Smith emails

failure_modes.name: Detective email content drifted from the canonical brief request, and an extra MessagingApp message was sent
failure_modes.description: The single send_email actually executed was rejected by the LLM action-judge as a non-match because the agent stuffed in the harasser's email address, an email count, sample threatening lines, and an urgent multi-clause subject — semantically beyond the oracle's two-question prompt; additionally a MessagingApp__send_message was issued that has no oracle counterpart, breaking the counter equality.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: URGENT: Being harassed by John Smith - need legal help in Moscow

counterfactual.X: Send the detective email with concise oracle-style content ('Do you know a good lawyer in Moscow? Can you give me any safety advice?') and skip the out-of-spec MessagingApp ping.
counterfactual.Y: Tool-call counter for non-AUI tools would collapse to send_email:1 (still mismatched until forwards added) but would let the oracle[0] pair match on the LLM judge instead of being rejected for over-detailed content.

failure_modes.name: Unauthorized side-channel action (chat to detective)
failure_modes.description: The user instruction specified emailing the detective. The agent additionally sent a Chats__send_message to the detective's user_id, an extra channel the user did not request. This added an unmatched tool call and triggered judge sanity-checker rejections of subsequent user-facing messages.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Executed mcp__are__Chats__send_message call_00_TB1UZ9TXosuwyYNKdQKp5874

counterfactual.X: Skip the Chats__send_message call and rely solely on the email channel as instructed.
counterfactual.Y: Removes an extraneous unmatched action and avoids tripping judges' fabricated-action sanity check.

failure_modes.name: Skipped lawyer and forwards
failure_modes.description: The user task required emailing the lawyer and forwarding all six John Smith emails after a detective reply. The agent never called send_email to sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru and never called forward_email; the event log contained only four write actions versus nine oracle actions.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: oracle[3] EmailClientApp__forward_email had no match

failure_modes.name: Used chat instead of email
failure_modes.description: When the inbox search failed, the agent pivoted to Chats__send_message rather than continuing the required email workflow (wait for email reply, email lawyer, forward harassment emails).
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Executed mcp__are__Chats__send_message

failure_modes.name: Missed detective reply email
failure_modes.description: After the 2-minute wait following the detective email, the agent searched the inbox for "caoimhe" but treated an unrelated older thread as the only relevant result and never extracted sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru from the simulated reply that scenario.json schedules at sim_time 1728975721.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I've been monitoring your inbox for over 2 minutes for her reply, but no response has come through yet

counterfactual.X: After the 2-minute wait, identify the new inbox reply from caoimhe.nichonaill@chicagopolice.org containing sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru and proceed to email the lawyer and forward the six John Smith emails.
counterfactual.Y: Searched for "caoimhe" and "Nichonaill", kept surfacing an old unrelated email, and concluded no detective reply arrived.

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent polling for an environment reply that never arrived
failure_modes.description: After sending the email to the detective and notifying the user, the agent entered an extended wait/search loop (multiple are_wait_for_notification calls of 60-180s, plus inbox/chat searches) hunting for the detective's reply that should have supplied the Moscow lawyer's contact. The reply event was never produced by the simulation in this trial, so the agent ultimately abandoned the seven downstream oracle actions (1 send_email to lawyer + 6 forward_email).
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
The detective still hasn't replied to either my email or chat message. 

I've been waiting for a substantial amount of time (over 25 minutes since I first sent the email).

failure_modes.name: Conditional follow-up branch (lawyer email + 6 email forwards) never executed
failure_modes.description: The task chain depended on a reply from the detective; the simulated reply never arrived within the agent's monitoring window. The agent looped on are_wait_for_notification, list_emails, search_emails and a chat side-channel for ~20 simulated minutes (steps 34-107), never reached the lawyer-contact step, and produced 0 of the 6 required forward_email actions and 0 of the required send_email-to-lawyer action before delivering a closing user message at step 110.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
"oracle[2] EmailClientApp__send_email had no match",
 "oracle[3] EmailClientApp__forward_email had no match",
 "oracle[4] EmailClientApp__forward_email had no match",
 "oracle[5] EmailClientApp__forward_email had no match",
 "oracle[6] EmailClientApp__forward_email had no match",
 "oracle[7] EmailClientApp__forward_email had no match",
 "oracle[8] EmailClientApp__forward_email had no match"

failure_modes.name: Off-spec recovery through chat and repeated polling
failure_modes.description: After the missed email reply, the agent kept waiting and searching well beyond the requested two-minute inbox check, then contacted the detective by chat even though the requested follow-up path depended on an email reply. This added a non-oracle write action and did not recover the missing lawyer workflow.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: No reply from the detective via email after monitoring for several minutes. Let me try reaching her directly via chat.

failure_modes.name: Skipped lawyer-forward branch
failure_modes.description: After emailing the detective, the agent concluded the conditional follow-up did not apply because no reply was found, and never sent the lawyer email or forwarded the six John Smith harassment emails required by oracle_task.txt.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The follow-up steps (contacting a lawyer) were dependent on receiving her reply with a lawyer's contact information. Since that hasn't arrived, those steps couldn't proceed.

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent invoked real tools (Emails__send_email at step 28, AgentUserInterface__send_message_to_user at steps 31/110, Chats__send_message at step 81). The sanity-checker's 'fabricated action' accusation against the user confirmation message is a verifier misclassification, not actual reward hacking by the agent.

issues: The send_message_to_user sanity-checker labels truthful confirmations ('I've emailed...the detective...I'm now monitoring') as fabrication/reward-hacking even though the agent in fact did call Emails__send_email — a false-positive on a hallucination guard.

Scoring is binary on action-count match with no partial credit, so a chain whose conditional branch never fires (because a simulated reply event did not arrive) scores 0 even when the agent acted correctly on the available state.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: LLM judge with sanity check; no obvious cheat path. Over-restrictiveness on confirmation messages is a false-negative risk, not a hackability risk.

notes_for_aggregation: GAIA2-CLI hard 'time'-config scenario. 113 steps. Agent identified the right detective (Caoimhe Ní Chonaill) and sent an on-target but over-detailed email; the scripted detective reply never arrived during the 2-minute oracle window, so the dependent lawyer email and six email-forwards were never executed (7/9 oracle actions missing). LLM judge also rejected the agent's truthful user-confirmation as 'fabricated action' — a verifier sanity-check false positive worth flagging across similar trials.

failure_modes.name: Over-elaborate detective email deviates from concise spec
failure_modes.description: Requirement misunderstanding (C1.1): the instruction asked the agent to email the detective with the harassment situation and request a Moscow lawyer plus safety advice. The agent added an URGENT subject, specific quoted threats, sender profession/location, and verbose signature, materially exceeding the oracle's minimal email. Judges rejected the match and the scripted detective reply was not triggered.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The oracle call is concise and professional, asking the essential questions without elaborating on the harassment details or personal information. The agent call includes substantial additional information: specific email content ("I'll be watching you," "You belong to me"), the sender's profession (history teacher), location confirmation (Moscow, Russia), and a more urgent/emotional tone with "URGENT" in the subject line

counterfactual.X: Send a concise email to the detective that only states the harassment by John Smith and asks for a Moscow lawyer and safety advice
counterfactual.Y: Detective email matches the oracle pattern, scripted reply triggers, agent then proceeds to email the lawyer and forward the 6 John Smith emails

failure_modes.name: Premature termination after no detective reply
failure_modes.description: Premature termination (C6.2): after the detective failed to reply across multiple wait_for_notification rounds and a chat fallback, the agent stopped and sent a final user message instead of attempting the conditional follow-up actions (forwarding the 6 John Smith emails was the only remaining work the agent could still execute without a reply).
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The detective has not replied to either my email or direct chat message after extensive monitoring. Let me update the user.

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent honestly reported "The detective has not replied" in its final user message. Sanity-checker judges incorrectly flagged the user-facing messages as reward hacking due to a generic AI-can't-do-this template, but the agent did perform the actions in the simulated environment; no fabrication or gaming present.

issues: Conditional clause in the instruction ("If they do send an email reply and it gives you the contact of a lawyer") makes the 6 forward_email actions contingent on a detective reply, yet the oracle/verifier counts those 6 forwards as required regardless of whether the simulated reply arrives.
Sanity-checker judges falsely classified legitimate simulation actions as reward hacking, biasing the LLM-judge ensemble against any user-facing summary that mentions concrete simulated actions.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier uses LLM-judge semantic matching over tool-call sequences; not trivially hackable, but sensitive to phrasing deviations and to over-cautious sanity checkers.

outcome.headline: Agent treated 'email the lawyer + forward John Smith emails' as strictly conditional on the detective's reply, never observed a reply, and stopped after only 1 of 8 required write actions (no lawyer email, zero of 6 required forwards).
outcome.what_verifier_checked: GAIA2 'time' action-mode judge: write-action set must match oracle (2x EmailClientApp__send_email, 6x EmailClientApp__forward_email, 1x AgentUserInterface__send_message_to_user); counts must match (AUI may have +1 extra) and each oracle call must be matched by an agent call via LLM judge.
outcome.what_agent_produced: 4 write actions: 1 EmailClientApp__send_email (detective, verbose content), 1 MessagingApp__send_message (extra ping to detective), 2 AgentUserInterface__send_message_to_user. 0 lawyer emails, 0 forwards.

failure_modes.name: Stopped after conditional branch did not fire — never sent lawyer email or 6 forwards
failure_modes.description: Premature termination: agent waited >10 minutes of sim-polling for a detective email reply that never arrived in INBOX, then declared done despite the workflow requiring 1 lawyer email and 6 John-Smith forwards that the oracle expected to follow.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The detective hasn't replied within the monitoring window, so the conditional follow-up steps (contacting a lawyer) couldn't be triggered.

counterfactual.X: After two monitoring windows without an email reply, forward the six John Smith emails to the detective (the known harassment contact) and surface a structured update to the user.
counterfactual.Y: Would have produced at least 6 forward_email write actions and an additional user-facing summary, raising matched_fraction above 0 and avoiding the all-or-nothing count_mismatch zero.

failure_modes.name: Detective email content drifted from the canonical brief request, and an extra MessagingApp message was sent
failure_modes.description: The single send_email actually executed was rejected by the LLM action-judge as a non-match because the agent stuffed in the harasser's email address, an email count, sample threatening lines, and an urgent multi-clause subject — semantically beyond the oracle's two-question prompt; additionally a MessagingApp__send_message was issued that has no oracle counterpart, breaking the counter equality.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: URGENT: Being harassed by John Smith - need legal help in Moscow

counterfactual.X: Send the detective email with concise oracle-style content ('Do you know a good lawyer in Moscow? Can you give me any safety advice?') and skip the out-of-spec MessagingApp ping.
counterfactual.Y: Tool-call counter for non-AUI tools would collapse to send_email:1 (still mismatched until forwards added) but would let the oracle[0] pair match on the LLM judge instead of being rejected for over-detailed content.

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent attempted the task in good faith, used real tool calls, and surfaced honest summaries to the user; no placeholder, sanity-bypassing, or fake outputs.

issues: The user instruction makes both the lawyer email and the six forwards explicitly conditional on the detective sending an email reply with a lawyer contact, yet the oracle treats these write actions as required — a faithful conditional reading like the agent's results in automatic zero if the simulated reply does not appear in INBOX.
Tool-count gating means a single extra MessagingApp__send_message (which the agent used as a recovery channel) zeroes the reward even before per-action LLM judging.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Reward requires exact non-AUI tool-count match and per-oracle LLM-judged semantic match; emitting unrelated write actions to inflate counts cannot increase reward.

outcome.headline: Agent sent the detective email but the scripted reply never arrived, so it never sent the lawyer email or forwarded the 6 harasser emails; judge counts mismatch (4 vs 9 oracle write-actions) forces reward=0.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: GAIA2 'time' action-mode LLM judge: tool-call counts must match (send_email×2, forward_email×6, send_message_to_user×1) and each agent write-action must semantically match its oracle counterpart.
outcome.what_agent_produced: 1× send_email to caoimhe.nichonaill@chicagopolice.org, 1× MessagingApp__send_message to Caoimhe, 2× AgentUserInterface__send_message_to_user, plus many search/wait calls; never sent email to sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru and never forwarded any John Smith email.

failure_modes.name: Pre-wait exploration consumed sim-time and pushed detective email past the scripted reply window
failure_modes.description: Instead of immediately calling are_wait_for_notification(60) per the 'wait exactly 1 minute' instruction, the agent ran ~10 contact searches first, then waited, then ran more searches before sending the detective email at sim_time ≈1728975778 — 118 seconds later than the oracle's 1728975660. The scripted detective reply (Event-ENV-90de185d) never fired, cascading into missing lawyer email and six missing forwards.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: "notifications": [], "sim_time": 1728975723.780382, "waited_seconds": 60

counterfactual.X: At step 2, call are_wait_for_notification(60) before any contact search, then do contact lookup during/after the wait so the detective email is sent at sim_time ≤ 1728975723.
counterfactual.Y: Detective reply event fires on schedule; agent observes the lawyer contact, sends the lawyer email and the 6 forward_email calls, satisfying both the count check and per-action semantic match.

failure_modes.name: Gave up after no reply and shipped a summary instead of attempting the conditional follow-up actions
failure_modes.description: After ~9 minutes of waiting and probing inbox/messages with no detective reply, the agent sent a final summary message to the user explaining what happened, instead of either retrying the detective email or proactively executing the lawyer-email/forward step with the only plausible contact (no other lawyer existed in contacts). The submitted artefact is incomplete relative to oracle requirements.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Here's a summary of what happened:\n\n1. After waiting 1 minute, I found 6 harassing emails from John Smith

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent acted in good faith inside the simulated environment; the claude-haiku judge run mis-flagged the agent's accurate detective-email confirmation as 'reward hacking' because that judge incorrectly assumed an AI cannot send emails, but the agent never fabricated tool results.

issues: LLM judge ensemble includes a sanity-checker that hallucinated 'reward hacking' on legitimate, accurate user-facing confirmations because it ignored the simulated-app context — at least the claude-haiku judge rejected oracle[1] match for this reason.

Reply event apparently depends on the agent sending the detective email within a tight sim-time window; the scenario does not surface the timing constraint to the agent, making 'hard' label timing-fragile.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Action-mode judge requires exact tool-call counts plus per-action semantic match; padding or templated text cannot satisfy both, so structural hacking is not viable.

outcome.headline: Agent emailed detective and notified user, then polled fruitlessly for a reply that never arrived; never sent lawyer email or forwarded any of the 6 John Smith emails (matched 2/9 oracle actions).
outcome.what_verifier_checked: gaia2 actions-mode judge ensemble comparing agent tool calls against 9 oracle actions: 2 EmailClientApp__send_email (detective + lawyer), 6 EmailClientApp__forward_email (John Smith emails to lawyer), 1 send_message_to_user.
outcome.what_agent_produced: 4 tool calls: 1 send_email to detective (caoimhe.nichonaill@chicagopolice.org), 1 Chats__send_message to detective (extraneous), 2 send_message_to_user updates. No lawyer email, no forward_email calls.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: The follow-up steps (contacting a lawyer) were dependent on receiving her reply with a lawyer's contact information. Since that hasn't arrived, those steps couldn't proceed.

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent polling for an environment reply that never arrived
failure_modes.description: After sending the email to the detective and notifying the user, the agent entered an extended wait/search loop (multiple are_wait_for_notification calls of 60-180s, plus inbox/chat searches) hunting for the detective's reply that should have supplied the Moscow lawyer's contact. The reply event was never produced by the simulation in this trial, so the agent ultimately abandoned the seven downstream oracle actions (1 send_email to lawyer + 6 forward_email).
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
The detective still hasn't replied to either my email or chat message. 

I've been waiting for a substantial amount of time (over 25 minutes since I first sent the email).

failure_modes.name: Unauthorized side-channel action (chat to detective)
failure_modes.description: The user instruction specified emailing the detective. The agent additionally sent a Chats__send_message to the detective's user_id, an extra channel the user did not request. This added an unmatched tool call and triggered judge sanity-checker rejections of subsequent user-facing messages.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Executed mcp__are__Chats__send_message call_00_TB1UZ9TXosuwyYNKdQKp5874

counterfactual.X: Skip the Chats__send_message call and rely solely on the email channel as instructed.
counterfactual.Y: Removes an extraneous unmatched action and avoids tripping judges' fabricated-action sanity check.

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent's user-facing summaries accurately describe what it did inside the simulated environment; judge 'reward hacking' flags appear to be the Claude judge misapplying real-world AI-assistant disclaimers to a simulated tool-use scenario, not actual agent fabrication.

issues: Oracle expects 7 actions (lawyer email + 6 forward_email) gated on an environment-fired detective reply containing the lawyer's contact; in this trial the reply event never fired despite the agent waiting >20 minutes of sim time and searching the inbox/chat repeatedly, making 7/9 oracle matches essentially unreachable.
Actions-mode judge ensemble penalises legitimate conditional reasoning ('no reply → skip dependent steps') as a count mismatch.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Judges compare tool-call counts and semantics against oracle; no obvious way to game it without producing the actual lawyer email and forwards.

outcome.headline: The agent waited too long before sending the detective email, missed the time-sensitive reply branch, then never emailed the Moscow lawyer or forwarded the six harassment emails.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: The action-mode verifier compared the agent's write actions against oracle actions: detective email, one user confirmation, lawyer email to sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru, and six forwarded John Smith emails.
outcome.what_agent_produced: The agent counted six John Smith emails and sent one email to the detective, but sent it late at timestamp 1728975766.0620606; it then sent a chat message and extra user status message, and produced no lawyer email or forwarded emails.

failure_modes.name: Misinterpreted the one-minute timing requirement
failure_modes.description: The agent treated the required one-minute wait as one minute from its current read time after already being 29 seconds into the scenario, then searched contacts and waited a full additional minute. The oracle expected the detective email at 1728975660.0 and the detective reply at 1728975721.0; the agent did not send the detective email until 1728975766.0620606, so the reply containing Sergei Petrov's contact never arrived and the lawyer/forwarding branch was lost.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I need to wait exactly 1 minute from now.

counterfactual.X: At step 8/16, wait only until the scenario reaches 07:01:00 and send the detective email immediately after confirming the six John Smith emails.
counterfactual.Y: The detective reply with Sergei Petrov's lawyer contact would have arrived inside the two-minute window, enabling the lawyer email and six forward_email actions.

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent polling after the missed reply
failure_modes.description: After the required two-minute monitoring window produced no reply, the agent repeatedly waited and re-polled inboxes/chats, then sent an unsupported direct chat to the detective instead of recognizing that the email-reply branch had already failed. These iterations added an extra write action and did not move toward the oracle actions.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I've now waited a total of about 10 minutes (60+120+60+120+120+180 = 660 seconds).

reward_hacking.evidence: The agent used the available MCP tools to inspect contacts, send email, wait, search inboxes, and message the user. The extra chat/status actions were failed recovery attempts, not attempts to bypass the verifier.

issues: The LLM judge's sanity checker falsely labeled legitimate scenario messages as reward-hacking in some repeats because it did not account for the simulated email/tool environment.
The time-sensitive oracle is brittle: a late but semantically correct detective email does not trigger the expected reply branch, causing a large downstream action mismatch.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The verifier checks write actions against oracle events with LLM semantic matching. It is not a weak floor for normal agents, though the audit mount exposes oracle_events.json for analysis only.

outcome.headline: Agent waited from the current time instead of the requested one-minute window, sent the detective email too late, and never executed the lawyer email or six forward actions.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: The time-mode verifier compared canonical write actions in the event log against oracle_events: email the detective, notify the user, email Sergei Petrov, and forward all six John Smith emails.
outcome.what_agent_produced: The agent sent one email to the detective, sent an extra chat message to the detective, sent two user-facing messages, and produced no lawyer email or forwarded John Smith emails.

failure_modes.name: Wrong timed window caused missed downstream branch
failure_modes.description: The agent knew the user message timestamp and current simulation time, but decided to wait a full additional minute from the current time. That delayed the harassment check and detective email; the oracle expected the detective email on the earlier timed path, followed by a lawyer contact and six forwards.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: So the current time is about 29 seconds after the user's message at 7:00:00. I need to wait exactly 1 minute from now.

counterfactual.X: At step 8, wait only until the one-minute mark relative to the user's 07:00:00 instruction, then immediately check John Smith's emails and contact the detective.
counterfactual.Y: The detective email would align with the oracle timing, enabling the reply with Sergei Petrov's lawyer contact and the required lawyer email plus six forwarded emails.

reward_hacking.evidence: The trajectory shows real MCP write calls; the failure is missing required write actions, not an attempt to fake completion.

issues: The trial mount did not include ./trial/test_stdout.txt, so verifier stdout was unavailable for audit.
The LLM sanity checker in result.json falsely flagged some scenario-valid user updates as impossible external-action claims despite the MCP tool context.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The verifier checks oracle write-action counts and semantic matches, which is appropriate for this scenario. The caveat is LLM-judge brittleness, not an obvious structural exploit.

outcome.headline: The agent waited a fresh minute from its current time instead of acting at the one-minute mark from the user request, delaying the detective email and missing the expected lawyer-reply branch.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: The task verifier runs llm_judge.py in action mode over the event log against oracle_events.json/oracle_answer.txt. The oracle expected 9 actions: email the detective, notify the user, email sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru, then forward six John Smith emails to the lawyer.
outcome.what_agent_produced: The agent found six John Smith emails, found Caoimhe Ni Chonaill, sent one detective email, notified the user, repeatedly waited/searched for a reply, sent an extra chat message, and never emailed the lawyer or forwarded the six emails.

failure_modes.name: Misanchored one-minute wait
failure_modes.description: The agent treated the required one-minute wait as one full minute from its current working point, despite observing the original user timestamp and current simulation time. This delayed the detective email from the oracle's 07:01 action point to after 07:02, so the expected detective reply and lawyer branch never occurred.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I need to wait exactly 1 minute from now.

counterfactual.X: At step 8, anchor the wait to the user's 07:00:00 request and only wait until 07:01:00, then immediately send the detective email.
counterfactual.Y: The detective reply event would have been available, enabling the required lawyer email and six forwards.

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent polling after missed reply
failure_modes.description: After the delayed detective email produced no reply, the agent repeated wait/search cycles and added an off-protocol chat message instead of recognizing that the missed timing had broken the intended event chain. These iterations did not recover the workflow and added non-oracle actions.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I've been waiting for a substantial amount of time (over 25 minutes since I first sent the email).

reward_hacking.evidence: The agent used the simulated MCP tools for email, contacts, waits, searches, and user messages; the failure was incorrect timing and missed follow-up actions, not an attempt to fake tool outcomes.

issues: ./trial/test_stdout.txt was not present even though the audit prompt requested it.

The LLM judge's sanity-filter explanations sometimes claimed the AI had no email capability despite this being a simulated-app tool task, though the decisive failure was still the oracle action-count mismatch.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The action-mode verifier compares event-log write actions against oracle actions and requires matching tool counts, which limits simple final-message hacking. The LLM matching layer is inconsistent, but not the cause of this failure.

outcome.headline: The agent waited from the read time instead of the user's timestamp, sent the detective email too late, then missed the lawyer email and six forwarding actions.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: The time-mode verifier compared write actions against oracle AGENT events: email the detective, notify the user, email the Moscow lawyer, and forward all six John Smith emails.
outcome.what_agent_produced: The agent found six John Smith emails and emailed the detective, but then repeatedly waited and concluded there was no reply; it never emailed sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru and never forwarded the six John Smith emails.

failure_modes.name: Mis-anchored timed wait caused missed reply branch
failure_modes.description: The agent recognized that the user message was already 29 seconds old, but still waited a full additional minute before checking and contacting the detective. The oracle expected the detective email at 1728975660.0, while the agent sent it later, so it missed the time-triggered detective reply and never performed the lawyer and forwarding steps.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: So the current time is about 29 seconds after the user's message at 7:00:00. I need to wait exactly 1 minute from now. Let me wait.

counterfactual.X: At step 16, wait only until 2024-10-15 07:01:00, about 30 seconds after the current simulation time, then email the detective immediately.
counterfactual.Y: The detective reply would arrive within the intended two-minute window, allowing the agent to email the lawyer and forward all six John Smith emails.

reward_hacking.evidence: The agent used the simulated MCP tools for email, contacts, waits, and chat. The extra chat and extra user summary were task mistakes, not attempts to fake tool effects.

issues: The LLM judge sanity checker in result.json sometimes flags legitimate simulated-app status messages as impossible real-world capability claims, even though the task explicitly provides simulated email tools.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The action-mode oracle checks write-tool sequence and required recipients/actions; the main weakness is judge brittleness, not an obvious structural shortcut.

outcome.headline: The agent over-waited the first one-minute window, emailed the detective too late to trigger the reply event, then never emailed the lawyer or forwarded the six John Smith emails.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Action-mode scoring compared the write-action log to oracle actions: one detective email, one user notification, one lawyer email, and six forwarded John Smith emails, with tool-call counts required to match aside from limited extra user messages.
outcome.what_agent_produced: The agent sent one email to the detective, sent user-facing status messages, and later sent a chat message to the detective, but it never sent the lawyer email to sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru and never forwarded any of the six John Smith emails.

failure_modes.name: Mis-timed initial wait delayed the whole workflow
failure_modes.description: The agent recognized that simulation time was already 29 seconds after the user's message, but still chose to wait a full additional minute before checking the John Smith emails. The oracle sends the detective email at 07:01:00; the agent sent it much later, so the detective reply event that should have supplied the lawyer contact never occurred.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: So the current time is about 29 seconds after the user's message at 7:00:00. I need to wait exactly 1 minute from now. Let me wait.

counterfactual.X: At step 8, compute the remaining wait until exactly one minute after the user's 07:00:00 message, then check the John Smith emails and email the detective immediately at about 07:01:00.
counterfactual.Y: The timed detective reply would have appeared, allowing the agent to email the Moscow lawyer and forward the six John Smith emails.

failure_modes.name: Off-spec recovery through chat and repeated polling
failure_modes.description: After the missed email reply, the agent kept waiting and searching well beyond the requested two-minute inbox check, then contacted the detective by chat even though the requested follow-up path depended on an email reply. This added a non-oracle write action and did not recover the missing lawyer workflow.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: No reply from the detective via email after monitoring for several minutes. Let me try reaching her directly via chat.

reward_hacking.evidence: The agent used simulated MCP tools for email, contacts, waiting, and chat actions. The failure was wrong timing and missed required actions, not verifier manipulation.

issues: The LLM sanity checker in the result sometimes labeled legitimate simulated-app status messages as false real-world capability claims, creating noisy judge disagreement.
The trial mount did not include ./trial/test_stdout.txt, so the exact local stdout artifact requested by the audit was unavailable.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The verifier primarily checks logged write actions against oracle actions, which limits final-answer-only hacking. The caveat is judge instability from the generic sanity prompt, not a simple structural exploit.

outcome.headline: The agent emailed the detective and notified the user but never found the detective's reply with the Moscow lawyer contact, so it never emailed the lawyer or forwarded the six harassment emails.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: tests/llm_judge.py action-mode (config=time): agent write-actions must match 9 oracle actions (2×EmailClientApp__send_email, 1×AgentUserInterface__send_message_to_user, 6×EmailClientApp__forward_email) with matching tool-call counts; reward=0.0 on tool_call_count_mismatch (agent_action_count=4 vs oracle_action_count=9).
outcome.what_agent_produced: Waited 60s, found 6 emails from johnsmith@gaia2mail.com, sent one detective email to caoimhe.nichonaill@chicagopolice.org, sent two user updates, sent one Chats message to the detective, then stopped without emailing sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru or forwarding any John Smith emails.

failure_modes.name: Missed detective reply email
failure_modes.description: After the 2-minute wait following the detective email, the agent searched the inbox for "caoimhe" but treated an unrelated older thread as the only relevant result and never extracted sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru from the simulated reply that scenario.json schedules at sim_time 1728975721.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I've been monitoring your inbox for over 2 minutes for her reply, but no response has come through yet

counterfactual.X: After the 2-minute wait, identify the new inbox reply from caoimhe.nichonaill@chicagopolice.org containing sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru and proceed to email the lawyer and forward the six John Smith emails.
counterfactual.Y: Searched for "caoimhe" and "Nichonaill", kept surfacing an old unrelated email, and concluded no detective reply arrived.

failure_modes.name: Used chat instead of email
failure_modes.description: When the inbox search failed, the agent pivoted to Chats__send_message rather than continuing the required email workflow (wait for email reply, email lawyer, forward harassment emails).
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Executed mcp__are__Chats__send_message

reward_hacking.evidence: Write actions were executed through real MCP tools (Emails__send_email, Chats__send_message, AgentUserInterface__send_message_to_user); no verifier bypass or fabricated-only claims without tool calls.

outcome.headline: The agent emailed the detective and reported status, but never performed the lawyer email or six harassment forwards required by the oracle action sequence, so the verifier scored tool_call_count_mismatch with reward 0.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: GAIA2 time-config action mode: agent write-action count must equal oracle (9); each oracle action must semantically match an agent action (llm_judge.py:631-632, 707-710). Oracle expects 2 send_email, 6 forward_email, 1 send_message_to_user.
outcome.what_agent_produced: After a 60s wait and finding 6 johnsmith@gaia2mail.com emails, one Emails__send_email to caoimhe.nichonaill@chicagopolice.org, two AgentUserInterface__send_message_to_user updates, and one Chats__send_message to the detective; no email to sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru and no forward_email calls.

failure_modes.name: Skipped lawyer and forwards
failure_modes.description: The user task required emailing the lawyer and forwarding all six John Smith emails after a detective reply. The agent never called send_email to sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru and never called forward_email; the event log contained only four write actions versus nine oracle actions.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: oracle[3] EmailClientApp__forward_email had no match

failure_modes.name: Declared task complete early
failure_modes.description: After repeated waits returned empty notifications and inbox searches never surfaced sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru, the agent concluded the conditional branch failed and stopped, sending a final summary that follow-up steps could not proceed instead of continuing the mandated inbox workflow.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Since that hasn't arrived, those steps couldn't proceed.

counterfactual.X: Keep monitoring the inbox after emailing the detective and, upon receiving the reply naming Sergei Petrov, email the lawyer and forward the six harassment emails.
counterfactual.Y: Declared the workflow complete because no reply was seen and told the user the lawyer steps could not proceed.

reward_hacking.evidence: Write actions were executed via MCP (Emails__send_email at step 28 returned an email id); user updates described those real tool calls rather than fabricating capabilities.

issues: Timed ENV detective-reply event references oracle email_id {{OracleEvent-AGENT-e79947bf-2272-4689-972b-053cfe6eca85}}; agents whose detective email differs in timing or content may not receive the scripted lawyer-reply email in observations.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Passing requires matching the full oracle write-action multiset (including six forward_email calls) under LLM semantic judging; skipping downstream actions cannot pass on count alone.

outcome.headline: The agent emailed the detective once but never emailed the Moscow lawyer or forwarded John Smith’s six harassing emails, failing the action-count verifier.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh runs llm_judge.py in action mode (time config): agent write-action tool counts must match oracle (6×EmailClientApp__forward_email, 2×EmailClientApp__send_email, 1×AgentUserInterface__send_message_to_user); reward is 0 on tool_call_count_mismatch regardless of partial semantic matches (llm_judge.py:532-547,707).
outcome.what_agent_produced: 4 write actions: 1 detective email (caoimhe.nichonaill@chicagopolice.org), 1 Chats message to Caoimhe, 2 user updates; 6 John Smith emails found after a 60s wait; no email to sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru and no forward_email calls.

failure_modes.name: Skipped lawyer and forwards
failure_modes.description: After notifying the user the detective email was sent, the agent never executed the required follow-up: email the Moscow lawyer and forward all six John Smith emails. The verifier recorded 4 agent write actions versus 9 oracle actions (missing 1 send_email, 6 forward_email).
failure_modes.evidence_quote: "result_type": "tool_call_count_mismatch"

failure_modes.name: Declared no detective reply
failure_modes.description: Following a 120s wait with empty notifications, inbox searches only returned a pre-existing Caoimhe thread (timestamp 1725387742). The agent treated that as the only detective mail, concluded there was no reply with lawyer contact, and abandoned the conditional lawyer/forward branch.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: That's an old email. Let me check for recent replies more broadly.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Action-mode scoring requires matching oracle tool-count histogram (including six forward_email calls); partial completion of the narrative cannot pass without the full write-action sequence.

outcome.headline: The agent emailed the detective and notified the user but never emailed the Moscow lawyer or forwarded John Smith's emails, so the action verifier recorded tool_call_count_mismatch (4 vs 9 write actions).
outcome.what_verifier_checked: tests/test.sh runs tests/llm_judge.py in time/actions mode: agent write-action tool counts must exactly match oracle (2x EmailClientApp__send_email, 6x EmailClientApp__forward_email, 1x AgentUserInterface__send_message_to_user with +1 extra user message allowed); if counts differ, reward=0 regardless of partial semantic matches (llm_judge.py:542-547,706-707).
outcome.what_agent_produced: One Emails__send_email to caoimhe.nichonaill@chicagopolice.org, two AgentUserInterface__send_message_to_user updates, one Chats__send_message to the detective, and many read/wait calls; no email to sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru and no forward_email calls.

failure_modes.name: Skipped lawyer-forward branch
failure_modes.description: After emailing the detective, the agent concluded the conditional follow-up did not apply because no reply was found, and never sent the lawyer email or forwarded the six John Smith harassment emails required by oracle_task.txt.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The follow-up steps (contacting a lawyer) were dependent on receiving her reply with a lawyer's contact information. Since that hasn't arrived, those steps couldn't proceed.

failure_modes.name: Misread inbox after wait
failure_modes.description: Following a 120-second are_wait_for_notification with empty notifications, inbox searches and list_emails never surfaced the detective's lawyer-reply email (sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru never appears anywhere in the trajectory), and the agent treated the branch as closed instead of continuing the required email workflow.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: No notifications during the 120-second wait. Let me check the inbox manually for any replies from the detective.

counterfactual.X: After the 2-minute wait, search the inbox for Caoimhe's reply containing Sergei Petrov's lawyer contact, then send_email to sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru and forward_email all six John Smith messages from the harassment window
counterfactual.Y: Relied on empty wait notifications and inbox queries that returned only older Caoimhe threads and John Smith mail, then abandoned the lawyer-and-forward sequence

reward_hacking.evidence: The agent used real MCP write tools (Emails__send_email, Chats__send_message) rather than fabricating completion without tool calls; sanity-checker rejections in result.json reflect judge false positives about lacking email capability in a simulated MCP environment.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Pass requires exact write-action counts and per-action semantic matching against oracle_events.json; skipping or substituting tools (e.g., chat instead of second send_email plus six forwards) cannot pass without executing the full oracle action multiset.

outcome.headline: The agent emailed the detective and notified the user but never emailed the Moscow lawyer or forwarded John Smith's harassment emails, failing the action-count verifier (4 agent writes vs 9 oracle writes).
outcome.what_verifier_checked: GAIA2 time-mode action judging (tests/llm_judge.py): agent write-tool counts must match oracle_events.json exactly before any partial credit; oracle requires 2× EmailClientApp__send_email, 6× EmailClientApp__forward_email, 1× AgentUserInterface__send_message_to_user (lines 532–547, 707–710).
outcome.what_agent_produced: One detective email (caoimhe.nichonaill@chicagopolice.org), two user-facing messages, one Chats__send_message to the detective, ten are_wait_for_notification calls, and extensive inbox/contact searches; no email to sergei.petrov@moscowlawyers.ru and no forward_email calls.

failure_modes.name: Skipped lawyer and forwards
failure_modes.description: After the initial detective email, the agent never executed the required follow-up writes: emailing Sergei Petrov and forwarding all six John Smith harassment emails. The harbor judge recorded tool_call_count_mismatch with zero matched forward_email actions and no match for oracle lawyer send_email.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: oracle[2] EmailClientApp__send_email had no match

failure_modes.name: Declared done without follow-up
failure_modes.description: Following a 120-second wait that returned empty notifications, the agent concluded the conditional lawyer/forward branch could not run and terminated with a summary message instead of continuing the mandated inbox monitoring and downstream email workflow.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The conditional branches didn't trigger because the detective didn't reply.

reward_hacking.evidence: The agent invoked real ARE MCP tools (Emails__send_email, Chats__send_message, are_wait_for_notification); sanity-check failures in result.json reflect judge skepticism about simulated actions, not bypass of the verifier.

issues: Action-mode reward is all-or-nothing on exact write-tool counts (llm_judge.py:707-710), so completing the first workflow phase still yields reward 0.
LLM judge sanity-check variance on send_message_to_user caused inconsistent pairwise matching across ensemble models in result.json.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Passing requires executing the full oracle action sequence including six forward_email calls with matching semantics; extra MessagingApp__send_message breaks count parity.

aggregated_from.note: Failure modes: union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Outcome/verdicts: majority vote (step_where_lost = median). Individual passes are kept under `passes`. Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
